isnad_matn.isnad_ar: حدثنا محمد بن إبراهيم بن إسحاق (رحمه الله)، قال: أخبرنا أحمد بن محمد الكوفي الهمداني البزاز، قال: أخبرنا المنذر بن محمد، قال: حدثنا جعفر بن سليمان، عن عبد الله بن الفضل، عن أبان بن عثمان الاحمر، عن أبان بن تغلب، عن سعيد بن جبير، عن ابن عباس

narrators.known_identity: Muhammad ibn Ibrahim ibn Ishaq al-Taleqani

narrators.name_ar: أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ الْكُوفِيُّ الْهَمَدَانِيُّ الْبَزَّازُ
narrators.name_en: Ahmad ibn Muhammad al-Kufi al-Hamadani al-Bazzaz

narrators.ambiguity_note: A transmitter in al-Saduq's chains, commonly identified as al-Mundhir ibn Muhammad
A transmitter in al-Saduq's chains, commonly identified as Ja'far ibn Sulayman
A transmitter in al-Saduq's chains, commonly identified as Abdullah ibn al-Fadl

narrators.known_identity: Aban ibn Uthman al-Ahmar, a prominent Shia narrator and companion of Imam al-Sadiq (AS)
Aban ibn Taghlib ibn Riyah, a prominent Shia scholar
Sa'id ibn Jubayr, a famous tabi'i scholar (d. 95 AH)
Abdullah ibn Abbas, cousin of the Prophet (d. 68 AH)

en.summary: This lengthy narrative from Ibn Abbas recounts the detailed Quranic story of Prophet Yusuf (Joseph) and his brothers in Egypt. Ya'qub sends his sons to buy food during a famine; Yusuf recognizes them but conceals his identity; Sham'un is detained; Binyamin is demanded; the king's measuring cup is planted; and Yusuf reveals himself. Gabriel teaches Ya'qub a supplication invoking Muhammad, Ali, Fatima, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn, restoring his sight. A report from Imam al-Sadiq notes Yusuf was punished for not dismounting for his father.
en.seo_question: What is the full story of Prophet Yusuf and his brothers in Islamic tradition?

ur.summary: ابن عباس سے مروی یہ طویل روایت سورۃ یوسف کی تفصیلی کہانی بیان کرتی ہے۔

zh.summary: 这段来自伊本·阿巴斯的长篇叙事讲述了先知优素福及其兄弟的故事。
zh.seo_question: 先知优素福及其兄弟的完整故事是什么？

translations.en: Muhammad ibn Ibrahim ibn Ishaq (may Allah have mercy on him) narrated to us. He said: Ahmad ibn Muhammad al-Kufi al-Hamadani al-Bazzaz informed us. He said: al-Mundhir ibn Muhammad informed us. He said: Ja'far ibn Sulayman narrated to us, from Abdullah ibn al-Fadl, from Aban ibn Uthman al-Ahmar, from Aban ibn Taghlib, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn Abbas, who said:

When the family of Ya'qub was afflicted with what afflicted the people -- a shortage of food -- Ya'qub (peace be upon him) gathered his sons and said to them: O my sons, I have been informed that good food is being sold in Egypt and that its owner is a righteous man who does not imprison people. So go to him and buy food from him, for he will be good to you, God willing.

So they prepared and journeyed until they reached Egypt. They entered upon Yusuf (peace be upon him), and he recognized them while they did not recognize him. He said to them: Who are you? They said: We are the children of Ya'qub ibn Ishaq ibn Ibrahim, the Friend of the Merciful, and we are from the land of Canaan. Yusuf said: You are then the descendants of three prophets, yet you are not wise, and you have neither dignity nor humility. Perhaps you are spies for one of the kings who have come to my country. They said: O king, we are not spies nor people of war. Had you known our father, you would have honored us, for he is a prophet of Allah and the son of His prophets, and he is grieving.

Yusuf (peace be upon him) said to them: What causes his grief when he is a prophet of Allah and the son of His prophets, and Paradise is his abode, and he looks upon you in such number and strength! Perhaps his grief is rather due to your foolishness, ignorance, lying, scheming, and plotting. They said: O king, we are neither ignorant nor foolish, and his grief did not come from us. Rather, he had a son who was the youngest among us, named Yusuf. He went out with us to hunt and a wolf ate him. Since then, he has been sorrowful, grieving, and weeping. Yusuf (peace be upon him) said to them: Are you all from one father? They said: Our father is one, but our mothers are different. He said: What compelled your father to send all of you? Could he not have kept one of you for his comfort and solace? They said: He did; he kept one of us, the youngest among us. He said: Why did he choose him from among you? They said: Because he is the most beloved of his children to him after Yusuf. So Yusuf (peace be upon him) said to them: I will detain one of you to remain with me. Return to your father and convey my greetings to him, and tell him to send me the son he has kept, so that he may inform me of his grief, the premature graying of his hair, his weeping, and the loss of his sight.

When he said this, they drew lots among themselves, and the lot fell on Sham'un, so he was ordered to be detained. When they bid farewell to Sham'un, he said to them: O my brothers, see what I have fallen into, and convey my greetings to my father. So they bid him farewell and journeyed until they reached the Levant. They entered upon Ya'qub (peace be upon him) and greeted him with a weak greeting. He said to them: O my sons, why do you greet me with a weak greeting, and why do I not hear the voice of my friend Sham'un among you? They said: O our father, we have come to you from the greatest king among the people. No one has been seen like him in wisdom, knowledge, humility, tranquility, and dignity. If you had a likeness, he would be your likeness. But we are a household created for tribulation. The king accused us and claimed he would not believe us until you send Binyamin with us, with a message from you, informing him of your grief, the premature graying of your hair, your weeping, and the loss of your sight. So Ya'qub (peace be upon him) suspected that this was a scheme of theirs. He said to them: O my sons, what a terrible habit is your habit! Every time you go out, one of you is lost. I will not send him with you.

When they opened their belongings, they found their goods had been returned to them without their knowledge. They went to their father joyfully and said: O our father, no one has seen a king more God-fearing than this one. He returned our goods to us out of fear of sin. These are our goods returned to us. We will provide for our family, protect our brother, and gain an extra camel-load; that is an easy measure. Ya'qub said: You know that Binyamin is the most beloved of you to me after your brother Yusuf. In him is my comfort, and in him I find solace from among all of you. I will not send him with you until you give me a solemn pledge by Allah that you will bring him back to me unless you are overwhelmed.

So Yahuda guaranteed him. They went out until they reached Egypt. They entered upon Yusuf (peace be upon him), and he said to them: Did you deliver my message? They said: Yes, and we have brought its response with this young man, so ask him whatever you wish. Yusuf said to him: What has your father sent you to me with, young man? He said: He sent me to convey his greetings to you, and he says: You sent to me asking about my grief, the premature graying of my hair, my weeping, and the loss of my sight. Surely, the most intensely grieving and fearful of people is the one who reflects most upon the Resurrection. The premature graying came to me because of remembering the Day of Resurrection. What made me weep and my eyes go white was grief over my beloved Yusuf. Your grief over my grief has reached me, as well as your concern for my matter, so may Allah reward you. You cannot bring me anything that would make me happier than hastening my son Binyamin back to me, for he is the most beloved of my children after Yusuf. Through him I find comfort in my loneliness and connection in my solitude, and hasten to me what will help me support my family.

When he said this, tears choked Yusuf (peace be upon him) until he stood and entered the house and wept for a while. Then he came out to them and ordered food for them, and said: Let the children of each mother sit at a table. So they sat down and Binyamin remained standing. Yusuf said to him: Why have you not sat down? He said: I have no brother from the same mother among them. Yusuf (peace be upon him) said to him: Did you not have a brother from your mother? Binyamin said to him: Yes. Yusuf (peace be upon him) said: What happened to him? He said: They claim that a wolf ate him. He said: How much have you grieved over him? He said: I have had twelve sons, and I derived all their names from his name. Yusuf (peace be upon him) said to him: I see that you have embraced women and smelled children after him! Binyamin said: I have a righteous father, and he told me: Marry, and perhaps Allah, Mighty and Majestic, will bring forth from you offspring that will fill the earth with glorification. Yusuf (peace be upon him) said to him: Come and sit at my table. The brothers of Yusuf said: Allah has indeed favored Yusuf and his brother to the point that the king has seated him with him at his table. So Yusuf (peace be upon him) ordered that the king's measuring bowl be placed in the bag of Binyamin.

So when they had prepared, an announcer called out: 'O caravan, you are indeed thieves!' They turned to them and said: 'What are you missing?' They said: 'We are missing the king's measuring bowl, and whoever brings it shall have a camel-load, and I guarantee that.' They said: 'By Allah, you certainly know that we did not come to cause corruption in the land, and we are not thieves.' And the law and ruling among them was that the thief would be enslaved and not have his hand cut off.
So he detained him. His brothers said when they found the measuring bowl in the bag of Binyamin:

'If he steals, a brother of his stole before.' But Yusuf kept it within himself and did not reveal it to them. He said: 'You are in a worse position, and Allah knows best what you describe.' They said: 'O al-Aziz, indeed he has a father who is an old man, so take one of us in his place. Indeed, we see you among the doers of good.' He said: 'Allah forbid that we should take anyone other than him with whom we found our goods. Indeed, we would then be wrongdoers.' So when they had despaired of him, they secluded themselves in private consultation. Their eldest said: 'Do you not know that your father has taken a covenant from you by Allah, and before, you failed in your duty regarding Yusuf? So I will never leave this land until my father permits me or Allah decides for me, and He is the best of judges. Return to your father and say: O our father, indeed your son has stolen, and we did not testify except to what we knew, and we could not guard against the unseen. And ask the city in which we were and the caravan in which we traveled, and indeed, we are truthful.'
When they returned to their father, they said that to him. He said: Indeed my son does not steal.
'Rather, your souls have enticed you to something, so patience is most fitting. Perhaps Allah will bring them to me all together. Indeed, He is the All-Knowing, the All-Wise.'

Then he ordered his sons to prepare for Egypt. So they journeyed until they reached Egypt. They entered upon Yusuf (peace be upon him) and gave him a letter from Ya'qub, seeking his compassion and asking him to return his son. When he looked at it, tears choked him, and he could not bear it, so he stood and entered the house and wept for a while. Then he came out to them and they said to him:
'O al-Aziz, adversity has touched us and our family, and we have come with goods of little worth. So give us full measure and be charitable to us. Indeed, Allah rewards the charitable.'
So Yusuf said to them:

'Do you know what you did with Yusuf and his brother when you were ignorant?' They said: 'Are you indeed Yusuf?' He said: 'I am Yusuf and this is my brother. Allah has certainly been gracious to us. Indeed, whoever fears Allah and is patient, then indeed Allah does not allow to be lost the reward of those who do good.' They said: 'By Allah, certainly Allah has preferred you over us, and indeed we have been sinners.' He said: 'No blame will there be upon you today. May Allah forgive you, and He is the most merciful of the merciful.'
Then he ordered them to return to Ya'qub (peace be upon him) and said to them:
'Go with this shirt of mine and cast it over the face of my father; he will recover his sight. And bring me your family, all of them.'

Then Gabriel (peace be upon him) descended upon Ya'qub (peace be upon him) and said: O Ya'qub, shall I not teach you a supplication by which Allah will restore your sight and return your two sons to you? He said: Yes. He said: Say what your father Adam said, so that Allah forgave him; and what Nuh said, so that the Ark settled on Judi and he was saved from drowning; and what your father Ibrahim, the Friend of the Merciful, said when he was cast into the Fire, so Allah made it cool and peaceful for him. Ya'qub (peace be upon him) said: And what is that, O Gabriel? He said: Say: O Lord, I ask You by the right of Muhammad, Ali, Fatima, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn, that You bring me Yusuf and Binyamin together, and that You restore my sight. Ya'qub (peace be upon him) had not finished this supplication before the bearer of glad tidings came, and he cast the shirt of Yusuf upon him, and his sight was restored. He said to them: Did I not tell you that I know from Allah what you do not know?
'They said: O our father, ask forgiveness for us of our sins. Indeed, we have been sinners. He said: I will ask forgiveness for you from my Lord. Indeed, He is the Forgiving, the Merciful.'

It is reported in a narration from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that he said: He delayed them until dawn. Ya'qub went to Egypt, and Yusuf went out to receive him. He was about to dismount for Ya'qub, then he remembered his status as king and did not do it. So Gabriel (peace be upon him) descended upon him and said to him: O Yusuf, indeed Allah, Mighty and Majestic, says to you: What prevented you from dismounting for My righteous servant? What were you thinking? Extend your hand. So he extended it, and light came out from between his fingers. He said to him: What is this, O Gabriel? He said: This means that no prophet will ever come from your loins, as punishment for what you did to Ya'qub when you did not dismount for him.

So Yusuf said: 'Enter Egypt, God willing, in security.' And he raised his parents upon the throne, and they fell down in prostration to him. And Yusuf said to Ya'qub: 'O my father, this is the interpretation of my dream of before. My Lord has made it reality' -- until His saying -- 'cause me to die as a Muslim and join me with the righteous.'

key_phrases.phrase_ar: بِحَقِّ مُحَمَّدٍ وَعَلِيٍّ وَفَاطِمَةَ وَالْحَسَنِ وَالْحُسَيْنِ
key_phrases.phrase_en: By the right of Muhammad, Ali, Fatima, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn

similar_content_hints.description: Parallel accounts of the story of Yusuf and his brothers found in Tafsir al-Qummi and other exegetical works.
Other narrations where prophets invoke the names of Muhammad and his family as tawassul.
Hadiths about consequences of disrespecting parents or righteous elders.
